title: DKIM, SPF & DMARC - email spam
excerpt: Hey there we are using Gmail as our email integration and more of our emails are ending up in clients/portential clients’ spam folders.  Is there guidance for us to follow for the new DKIM, SPF and DMARC rules that cam i…

posts.author: JulesD (Julie Derevycka)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey there we are using Gmail as our email integration and more of our emails are ending up in clients/portential clients’ spam folders. Is there guidance for us to follow for the new DKIM, SPF and DMARC rules that cam in last month. Thanks J

posts.author: dvdsmpsn (David @ David Simpson Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you have not already added any DKIM, SPF and DMARC entries into your DNS, take a look at https://dmarcly.com/tools/ and get an idea of the values you should be setting for each of these.

As an example, an SPF record like so…

v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com include:sendgrid.net include:amazonses.com ~all

…should give permission to Google, SendGrid and Amazon AWS to send emails on your behalf.
